title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: Complete the text with ONE word only.

content.text: Beneath the surface of almost every forest floor lies a complex network of fungal threads known (0) mycelium. This hidden web acts as a biological communication system, allowing trees to share nutrients and even warn one (9) ANOTHER of incoming pests. (10) WERE it not for this 'Wood Wide Web', many forests would be far less resilient to environmental stress. || (11) IT is often observed that older trees, or 'mother trees', use this network to support younger saplings. Not (12) ONLY does this interaction increase the survival rate of the forest, but it also maintains genetic diversity. (13) DESPITE the importance of this system, it remains largely invisible to the casual observer. (14) AS far as scientists can tell, these fungal relationships have existed for millions of years. However, (15) SUCH is the fragility of this balance that modern deforestation can destroy it in an instant. (16) SO long as we protect our soil, this ancient network will continue to sustain life on Earth for future generations.

questions.explanation: Part of the reciprocal pronoun 'one another'.
Inversion in a second conditional (Were it not for...).
Dummy subject for a passive reporting structure.
Part of the negative inversion 'Not only... but also'.
Preposition of contrast followed by a noun phrase.
Part of the fixed phrase 'As far as'.
Inversion for emphasis to show degree: 'Such is the... that'.
Part of the fixed conditional expression 'so long as', meaning 'as long as' or 'provided that'.
